edible_parts: Root (sweet, raw or cooked) and seeds (spice).
other_uses: Slender Western meadow plant with lacy white flower umbels over clusters of sweet tubers.
prep: Eat the crisp sweet tubers raw or cooked (one of the finest wild roots, a top Western food) and the seeds as a caraway-like spice. CAUTION: learn it well — the carrot family includes deadly poison-hemlock and water-hemlock.
soil: Moist meadows drying by summer; well-drained.
risks: CAUTION: deadly Apiaceae look-alikes — eat only confirmed plants.
buy: Native-plant nurseries and native seed suppliers.
